title: Initial participant calculation
description: How to calculate income from a forestry managed investment scheme - initial participant.

items: Did you receive thinning proceeds from your forestry interest?

content_blocks.text: Yes − Include the actual amount you received at row d in Worksheet 2 . Go to step 2.

items: Did you receive sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you no longer hold?

content_blocks.text: Yes − Include the market value of the forestry interest at the time of the CGT event at row e in Worksheet 2 if the following applies:

items: a CGT event happened and you no longer held your forestry interest as a result of the CGT event (for example, because you had sold the interest to another person, or you had received harvest proceeds), and

you claimed a deduction, or can claim a deduction, or would be entitled to claim a deduction by for a CGT event happening within four years after the end of the income year in which you first pay an amount under the FMIS.

content_blocks.text: Yes − Include the amount by which the market value of your forestry interest was reduced at row f in Worksheet 2 if the following applies:

items: a CGT event happened and you still held your forestry interest (for example, because you have sold part of your interest or there was a partial harvest), and

you claimed a deduction, or can claim a deduction, or would be entitled to claim a deduction but for a CGT event happening within four years after the end of the income year in which you first pay an amount under the FMIS.

rows: Sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you no longer hold (from Step 2)
Sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you still held (from Step 3)
Add row d and row e and row f

content_text:
- Did you receive thinning proceeds from your forestry interest?
No − Go to step 2.
Yes − Include the actual amount you received at row d in Worksheet 2 . Go to step 2.
- Did you receive sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you no longer hold?
No − Go to step 3.
Yes − Include the market value of the forestry interest at the time of the CGT event at row e in Worksheet 2 if the following applies:
- a CGT event happened and you no longer held your forestry interest as a result of the CGT event (for example, because you had sold the interest to another person, or you had received harvest proceeds), and
- you claimed a deduction, or can claim a deduction, or would be entitled to claim a deduction by for a CGT event happening within four years after the end of the income year in which you first pay an amount under the FMIS.
No − Go to step 4.
Yes − Include the amount by which the market value of your forestry interest was reduced at row f in Worksheet 2 if the following applies:
- a CGT event happened and you still held your forestry interest (for example, because you have sold part of your interest or there was a partial harvest), and
- you claimed a deduction, or can claim a deduction, or would be entitled to claim a deduction but for a CGT event happening within four years after the end of the income year in which you first pay an amount under the FMIS.
Row | Description | Amount
d | Thinning proceeds (from Step 1) | $
e | Sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you no longer hold (from Step 2) | $
f | Sale and harvest receipts from a forestry interest you still held (from Step 3) | $
g | Add row d and row e and row f | $
